prompt: I need to build a 5-year SaaS revenue model from scratch. Here are the assumptions: we start Year 1 with 500 customers paying $200/month each. We expect 20% customer growth YoY and 5% annual price increases. Monthly churn is 2%. Build me the full IDFA-compliant model specification — every Named Range, every formula — before writing anything to Excel.
assertions: Every input assumption has a Named Range with Inp_ prefix (e.g., Inp_Customers_Y1, Inp_Price_Monthly, Inp_Customer_Growth, Inp_Price_Increase, Inp_Churn_Monthly)
All calculation formulas reference Named Ranges only — zero cell-coordinate references like A1, B2, $C$3
The model separates into three layers: Assumptions (inputs only), Calculations (logic only), Output (presentation only)
Revenue calculations correctly account for churn reducing the customer base
The specification includes both MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) as Named Ranges
Each formula is readable as a plain-English business rule without needing to trace cell references
Year-over-year naming convention uses _Y1, _Y2 etc. dimension suffixes consistently
